summary: Permanent Regulatory Affairs Specialist role supporting Medica Europe's European medical-device portfolio in Lyon. The position centres on EU MDR technical documentation, regulatory submissions, notified-body interactions, change assessment, clinical and post-market documentation, and traceable cross-functional coordination. Sofia offers directly verified evidence across these central responsibilities and exceeds the stated two-year experience threshold.
responsibilities: Prepare and maintain EU MDR technical documentation.
Coordinate submission evidence, notified-body responses and current submission plans.
Contribute to clinical evaluation, post-market surveillance, vigilance and risk-management documentation.
Improve regulatory processes while coordinating Quality, Clinical, Product Development and Manufacturing stakeholders.
company_details: Cross-functional environment spanning Quality, Clinical, Product Development and Manufacturing
cv_angle: Present a medical-device regulatory affairs specialist with three years of directly relevant experience, foregrounding EU MDR technical documentation, Class II devices, submission readiness, notified-body coordination and lifecycle compliance evidence.
letter_angle: Build the primary argument around Sofia's end-to-end EU MDR documentation and submission support, then show how her clinical, post-market and risk-management work enables traceable coordination across the functions named in the offer.
adaptation_guidance: Lead with verified EU MDR, Class II technical-documentation and notified-body evidence.
Make the three years of medical-device regulatory experience explicit to answer the experience threshold.
Use the skills section for recruiter-recognizable regulatory, quality-system and product-safety capabilities rather than general behaviours.

Demonstrate English technical writing through the English application and documented regulatory-writing responsibilities without adding an unsupported proficiency claim beyond the verified language record.
Do not create a project section because the candidate's Project Lab policy permits zero visible projects.

evidence_mappings.rationale: The candidate has three years of medical-device compliance experience spanning regulatory affairs and quality assurance.
Direct evidence confirms preparation and maintenance of EU MDR technical documentation for Class II devices.
The same experience explicitly includes change-impact assessments within EU MDR documentation work.
Direct evidence covers traceable submission evidence and notified-body questions through closure.
Direct evidence covers clinical evaluation, post-market surveillance, vigilance and risk-management updates.

Maintaining documentation, closing notified-body questions and supporting controlled quality follow-up provide relevant process-discipline evidence, but no candidate fact explicitly claims ownership of continuous-improvement initiatives.
EU MDR is demonstrated in regulatory documentation work and ISO 13485 in quality-record and CAPA review.
The source-backed experience establishes technical regulatory writing, while the verified language block records English at C1.

Concurrent documentation, submission, post-market and cross-functional activities indicate transferable prioritisation capability, although no exact fact explicitly evaluates multi-priority management.
The candidate coordinated quality, clinical and engineering evidence while maintaining traceability and closing notified-body questions.
The evidence directly covers Class II devices, CAPA, controlled documentation and notified-body interactions.
English C1 and direct coordination across quality, clinical and engineering teams support both parts of the requirement.

adaptation_decisions.decision: Lead with the candidate's EU MDR technical-documentation and submission-readiness record for Class II medical devices, including change-impact assessment and notified-body response coordination.
adaptation_decisions.rationale: This is the strongest coherent evidence cluster for the role's central obligations and differentiating ATS signals, while directly proving the experience threshold and day-to-day regulatory contribution.

adaptation_decisions.decision: Use clinical evaluation, post-market surveillance, vigilance and risk-management updates as the complementary argument for lifecycle compliance and cross-functional effectiveness.
adaptation_decisions.rationale: This cluster demonstrates breadth beyond initial submissions and aligns with the offer's recurring product-safety and lifecycle responsibilities without diluting the primary documentation argument.

project_plan.rationale: The candidate's Project Lab policy sets both minimum and maximum visible projects to zero, so no personal-project section may be introduced.

skill_plan.rationale: The visible competency plan prioritises exact, supported regulatory, quality and product-safety signals from the offer. It retains central EU MDR, documentation, submission, lifecycle-safety and English-writing terms while leaving general behaviours to experience evidence and the letter.
